originalTitle: UN passes resolution naming slave trade ‘gravest crime against humanity’
title: A Big, Important Story from Our World!
content:
Hello, hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, bringing you a very important story that talks about our history and our future. Grab your listening ears, because this is something we all need to understand, even if it makes us feel a little sad about the past. But don't worry, it also helps us make our world a much better and kinder place today and tomorrow!

What Happened?

There's a very big group called the United Nations, or the UN for short. Imagine all the countries in the world coming together like a giant school assembly! Well, the UN had a very special meeting. They talked about something that happened a long, long time ago, called the slave trade. And guess what? They made a very important announcement. They said that the slave trade was the 'gravest crime against humanity.' That's a big sentence, but it just means it was the very, very worst kind of bad thing that anyone could ever do to other people. It was so unfair and so wrong.

What Was the Slave Trade?

Now, let's talk about what the slave trade was. Imagine someone taking you away from your home, far, far away, and telling you that you don't belong to yourself anymore. They would make you work very hard, all day long, and you wouldn't get to choose what you do or where you go. You couldn't even see your family or friends. This is what happened to millions of people, especially from our beautiful continent of Africa, many, many years ago. People were captured from their homes in Africa and forced onto big ships. These ships then sailed across a giant ocean called the Atlantic Ocean. It was a very long and scary journey. When they arrived in new lands, they were made to work for other people, without being paid and without any freedom. They were treated very, very badly, like they were not important. Did you know? The Atlantic Ocean is one of the biggest oceans in the world! Imagine trying to sail across it in a small boat – it would take a very, very long time and be very dangerous.

Why Was It So, So Bad?

It was so bad because everyone deserves to be free. Everyone deserves to choose their own path, to live with their family, to laugh, and to learn. The slave trade took away all of these things from people. It took away their freedom, their happiness, and sometimes even their lives. It was not fair, it was not kind, and it caused so much pain and sadness that we still remember it today. Imagine if your best friend was suddenly taken away, and you couldn't play with them anymore, and you knew they were sad and working all the time. That's a tiny bit like how awful the slave trade was. It hurt so many families and communities.

Who Are the United Nations?

So, who exactly are the United Nations, this big group that made the important announcement? The UN is like a giant club where almost all the countries in the world are members. Their main job is to help all the countries work together, like good friends, to keep peace, solve problems, and make sure everyone is treated fairly and kindly. They want to make sure no one is left behind and that everyone has a chance to live a happy and healthy life. Did you know? The United Nations has a special building in a city called New York, far away, where all the countries meet and talk. They also have offices all over the world, helping people in different ways!

What Did the UN Say Now?

In their recent meeting, the UN made a very special rule, like a very important statement, called a 'resolution.' This resolution said very clearly that the slave trade, especially the one that crossed the Atlantic Ocean, was one of the worst things ever to happen in human history. It said it was a crime, not just against one person, but against all of humanity. This means it hurt everyone, everywhere, because it was so wrong. They want to make sure that everyone remembers this sad part of history. Why? So that we can learn from it and make sure nothing like it ever happens again. It's like learning from a mistake so you don't repeat it.

Who Agreed and Who Didn't?

Many, many countries listened to this important message. A huge number of countries, 123 of them, stood up and said, "Yes! We agree! The slave trade was a terrible crime." Imagine 123 hands all going up at once! That's a lot of countries agreeing that this was a very important truth. But a few countries, just three of them, did not agree with the message completely. Two of these countries were the United States and Israel. Even though they had different ideas about the resolution, the most important thing is that most of the world stood together to say that taking away people's freedom and treating them like objects is always, always wrong.

Why is This Important for Africa?

This story is very important for us in Africa because it talks about our ancestors, the people who lived here before us. Many of our ancestors were taken during the slave trade. It is part of our history, and understanding our history helps us understand who we are today and how strong and resilient we are. It reminds us of the brave people who fought for freedom and fairness. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, languages, and cultures! We have so much rich history and so many amazing stories of courage and hope.

What Can We Learn?

So, what can we learn from this big announcement? We learn that everyone, no matter where they come from or what they look like, deserves to be treated with kindness, respect, and freedom. We learn that it's super important to stand up for what is right and to speak out against unfairness. We learn that we should always celebrate our differences and treat everyone like a friend. This resolution helps us remember the past, but it also helps us look to the future. It helps us build a world where everyone is free, where everyone is safe, and where everyone can be happy. It reminds us that working together and being kind is the most powerful thing we can do.

Let's Build a Kind Future!

So, my friends, remember this important message from the United Nations. Let's promise to be kind to each other, to celebrate our freedom, and to always remember that everyone deserves to be treated with love and respect. We can all be heroes by choosing kindness every single day! Keep shining bright, my African stars!
summary: The United Nations recently announced that the slave trade, especially the one that took people from Africa across the Atlantic Ocean, was the very worst kind of crime against people, a message supported by most countries.
whyMatters: This is important because it helps us remember a very sad part of history so we can learn from it, making sure everyone today and tomorrow is treated with kindness, freedom, and fairness, especially for people in Africa whose ancestors suffered so much.
